page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance mostly in the average tier. The Intel Xeon processor delivers average single-core and multi-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1248.0 and a multi-core score of 4693.0. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong contrast: cached small-block reads reach an average of 465.3 GB/sec, whereas uncached large-block reads drop to a weak 37.76 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression workloads perform adequately, with single-threaded gzip compression averaging 27.0 GB/sec and Passmark encryption scoring 3858.83.

The instance exhibits performance limitations in specific areas. Passmark database operations and physics tests score in the bottom 25%, indicating potential bottlenecks for complex relational databases or heavy physics simulations. Additionally, LLM inference on a medium 7B model is poor, yielding only 5.5 tokens/sec for text generation. However, with 64.0 GB of RAM and dedicated CPU allocation, the instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local storage, such as caching layers, in-memory data processing, static web serving, and lightweight software compilation.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ce4.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, it provides 64.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark results show average CPU and compression performance, alongside a high cached memory read bandwidth of 465.3 GB/sec. However, uncached memory reads and database operations perform weakly. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory caching, static web serving, and general-purpose applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio without requiring high-speed local storage.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive applications and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at 3.2 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM hypervisor virtualization
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average performance in compression, web serving, and cached memory reads
Suited for in-memory caching, static web serving, and memory-heavy applications
